title: Unable to switch back to wsl after switching podman machine to use hyperv
body:
### Issue Description

i was originally running Podman on WSL and intentionally switched to Hyper-V to try
     and resolve a networking conflict. That experiment failed, and now i am  unable to switch Podman back to
     using WSL.  which i dont want because hyperv everything requires elevation when you interact with podman on hyperv 

   * A summary of the troubleshooting steps we've tried:
       * Setting vmtype = "wsl" in C:\Users\ChrisMcGrath\.config\containers\containers.conf.
       * Setting the CONTAINERS_MACHINE_PROVIDER environment variable to wsl.
       * Completely uninstalling and reinstalling Podman using winget.
       * Running wsl --update and wsl --install --no-distribution.
       * There are no other WSL distributions installed and i even tried removing the existing
         Ubuntu WSL install we had that is unrelated to the pod machine
nothing i could find onlne seems to work to get podman to use wsl again 

I am running windows 11 pro latest update and also podman pro latest (5.7) 
podman is also the only container engine running on my computer atm 

### Steps to reproduce the issue

Steps to reproduce the issue
1. install normal podman config 
2. switch to using hyperv 
3. try to switch back 


### Describe the results you received

Describe the results you received
before i uninstalled hyperv would tell me running podman machine on hyperv required elevation 
attempting set machine 

### Describe the results you expected

for podman machine to use wsl 

### podman info output

```yaml
podman 5.7
```

### Podman in a container

No

### Privileged Or Rootless

Rootless

### Upstream Latest Release

Yes

### Additional environment details

Additional environment details

### Additional information

I think i may have found what is actually happening after writing all of this out 

when you remove hyperv its leaves some files behind in program data 

not sure why podman is ignoring containers.conf and using the hyperv program data to decide to require hyperv,  but after deleting the program data folder it seems to have fixed the problem with it looking for hyperv after hyperv is uninstalled and reporting the error that it cont find hyper v and asking if it is installed  

still opening this in case other people run into this problem,  and this still seems like unintended behavior ,  pretty sure the intended behavior would be that podman machine should respect containers.conf 

simply having hyperv installed shouldn't be a detriment to how podman functions anyways again container.conf should be the source of truth

comments.body: i think the problem is soley the containers.conf issue not files leftover.  i do this switching all the time as a dev.  lemme check a windows box I might have running.  I think the path is incorrect but need to verify.
on my windows, `containers.conf` needs to sit in `C:\Users\me\AppData\Roaming\containers` and then it was honored.  thanks @l0rd for clarification.

One thing to note is that the new installer (the new one that installs Podman in `$env:localappdata\programs`) writes a config file in:

```
$env:appdata\containers\containers.conf.d\99-podman-machine-provider.conf
```

and overrides the content of the user config file

```
$env:appdata\containers\containers.conf
```

That may confuse users.

Winget, however, uses the legacy Windows installer, which creates the config file in
```
$env:programdata\containers\containers.conf.d\99-podman-machine-provider.conf
```
which has a lower priority compared to the user config file.

ya i was using the winget version , the files i deleted to get things working again were in the hyper-v program_data directory not the container directory for further context i had issues with port forwarding and gemini cli couldnt figure out the issue so it tried to switched me to hyper-v to try and fix the port problem that didnt resolve the issue i was having and hyper-v requires elevation and hadnt fixed the problem so i tried to switch back to wsl since i didnt really need hyper-v if it wasnt solving the issue i tried to reinstall podman and i originally had podman desktop installed but when i reinstalled i installed just the cli from winget i dont even get a containers.conf.d directory there , only containers.conf not sure which version installs the conf.d directory but i cont seem to find that in any of the documented directories for podman or appdata i ended up switching back docker temporarily , have time to debug this issue on my current deadline , going to try this again in the near futre tho my appdata containers directory this is the directory i deleted that got things working again doesnt seem to be anything overly remarkable about the directory other than some datafiles
i do however have .config/containers but it is also set to wsl as well it has a hype-v directory as well but doesnt seem to have impacted anything

After installing Podman using `winget` (or using Podman Desktop) the config file is in `C:\ProgramData\containers\containers.conf.d`:

```powershell
PS C:\Users\mario> ls C:\ProgramData\containers\containers.conf.d\
Get-ChildItem: Cannot find path 'C:\ProgramData\containers\containers.conf.d\' because it does not exist.
PS C:\Users\mario> winget install -e RedHat.Podman
Found Podman [RedHat.Podman] Version 5.7.1
(...)
Successfully installed
PS C:\Users\mario> ls C:\ProgramData\containers\containers.conf.d\

    Directory: C:\ProgramData\containers\containers.conf.d

Mode                 LastWriteTime         Length Name
----                 -------------         ------ ----
-a---          12/17/2025  1:29 PM             27 99-podman-machine-provider.conf
```
